instructions: Read the article and answer the questions to learn more about future tech.
title: Effective Communication

content.title: What Makes Communication Effective?

slides.content: Did you know 70% of workplace problems stem from poor communication?
Strong communication builds trust, reduces conflict, and helps you succeed in teams.
Effective communication = clear message + active listening + right tone/body language.
Instead of 'You’re wrong,' say: 'I see it differently — can I share my perspective?'

✔️ Be clear
✔️ Listen actively
✔️ Match words with tone/body

knowledge_check.question: Which is the best example of effective communication?
knowledge_check.reinforcement: B shows active listening and respect. A interrupts, C signals disinterest.

content.closing_hook: Next: How active listening changes conversations.

deep_dive.title: HBR: What Great Listeners Actually Do

content.title: The Power of Active Listening

slides.content: Ever felt ignored in a conversation?
Listening well shows respect and makes others more likely to listen to you.
Active listening = focusing fully, paraphrasing, asking clarifying questions.
Friend: 'I’m stressed about exams.' You: 'It sounds like you’ve got too much on your plate — want to talk through a plan?'

✔️ Don’t interrupt
✔️ Reflect back
✔️ Ask clarifying questions

knowledge_check.question: Which response shows active listening?
options: A) 'Relax, it’s not a big deal.'
B) 'You’re stressed, right? Want to talk about it?'
knowledge_check.reinforcement: B reflects feelings and invites sharing. A dismisses, C avoids the issue.

content.closing_hook: Next: How body language speaks louder than words.
citations: Brownell, J. (2012). Listening: Attitudes, Principles, and Skills. Pearson.

deep_dive.title: Greater Good Science Center: The Art of Listening

content.title: Body Language Speaks

slides.content: Over 60% of communication is nonverbal.
Your posture, gestures, and facial expressions can strengthen or weaken your words.
Good body language = eye contact, open posture, nodding, matching tone to message.
Saying 'I’m confident' with slouched shoulders won’t convince anyone.

✔️ Use eye contact
✔️ Keep posture open
✔️ Align words + tone + body

knowledge_check.question: Which body language shows openness?
options: A) Crossed arms, no eye contact
knowledge_check.reinforcement: B signals attentiveness. A shows defensiveness, C shows disinterest.

content.closing_hook: Next: Giving feedback that motivates, not frustrates.

content.title: Feedback Without Friction

slides.content: Why do some feedback conversations end in arguments?
Feedback done right helps growth; done wrong, it damages trust.
Use 'I' statements, be specific, and focus on behavior (not personality).
Instead of 'You’re lazy,' say: 'I noticed the report was late — let’s find ways to meet the deadline next time.'

✔️ Be specific
✔️ Focus on actions
✔️ Use constructive tone

knowledge_check.question: Which is better feedback?
options: B) 'I noticed the data had errors, let’s review together.'
knowledge_check.reinforcement: B addresses behavior constructively. A & C attack the person.

content.closing_hook: Final lesson: Putting it all together for confident communication.
citations: Stone, D., Heen, S., & Patton, B. (2010). Difficult Conversations. Penguin.

content.title: Confident Communication

slides.content: Confidence in communication isn’t about speaking louder — it’s about clarity and respect.
When you combine clarity, active listening, body language, and feedback skills, you inspire trust.
Confident communicators balance assertiveness with empathy. They state views clearly while inviting others in.
In a team: 'I believe this idea works best — what do you all think?'

✔️ Be clear and concise
✔️ Listen and acknowledge
✔️ Speak with empathy

knowledge_check.question: Which best shows confident communication?
options: A) Speaking loudly and ignoring input
B) Stating your view and inviting feedback
C) Staying silent to avoid conflict
knowledge_check.reinforcement: B combines assertiveness and empathy. A is domineering, C is passive.

content.closing_hook: You’ve built a foundation in communication! Next up: applying these skills in problem-solving and teamwork.
citations: Carnegie, D. (1990). The Quick and Easy Way to Effective Speaking. Pocket Books.

deep_dive.title: TED: Your Body Language Shapes Who You Are
